description:
Federation shipped as a bolt-on 'mode' (master_url): its own discovery branch, a second checkout mechanism, a config-symlink seam. A design review concluded the intent is sound but the model should be unified, not moded.

Converged target:

ONE mechanism. A tracker address (state_url, state_branch) -- always present, implicit default (code origin, balls/tasks) -- is materialized as a clone at .balls/state-repo. 'Standalone' is the case where state_url is the code's own origin; 'federated' is where it is a shared tracker. No mode, no discovery branch, no worktree-vs-clone split. 'bl remaster' just rewrites the address; --detach points it back at self.

Config ownership SPLIT, not merge. config.json stays a real file in the workspace, repo-owned (the tracker address, target_branch, delivery, review.pre_check, require_remote_*). A new project.json lives on the tracker branch, project-owned (version, id_length, min_bl_version, plugins), inherited by every workspace, and WINS on overlap so ownership is enforced, not advisory. The master.json pointer retires -- the address folds into config.json. Plugin config is project config; plugin auth and BALLS_IDENTITY stay the user's.

Terminology: 'tracker' hosts the shared branch; 'workspace' is where bl runs and task worktrees span from. External issue systems are 'external trackers'.

Quality bar, non-negotiable: the text-mergeable schema guarantees disjoint-field merges are clean -- full stop. One compact line per top-level field; same-field edits go to the resolver. No 'if we find churn' hedges. Conformance gates every refactor.

Children: the SPEC sweep, then the implementation refactors it gates.
title: Unified tracker-state model
